extra.reasoning:
rash appears in only two contexts: it is absent from every rule except r8, and r8 (priority 100, the highest priority) requires the conjunction of all 12 inputs leg_swelling, dyspnea, back_pain, fever, syncope, vision_loss, severe_headache, pallor, sweating, chest_pain, dizziness, AND rash (palpitations is not in the conjunction), and it sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity).

Take the witness input with all of leg_swelling, dyspnea, back_pain, fever, syncope, vision_loss, severe_headache, pallor, sweating, chest_pain, dizziness = true and rash = true (palpitations false).

- rash = true (the witness): all 12 conjuncts of r8 are satisfied, so r8 fires at priority 100 — higher than any other rule — yielding acuity = self_care.
- rash = false (same input otherwise): r8 does NOT fire (its conjunction is broken). The remaining firing rules include the priority-3 emergent rules r3/r4/r5/r7/r1 (pallor, fever, chest_pain, dyspnea, vision_loss), so the highest-priority winner gives acuity = emergent.

Since self_care < emergent in the listed enum order [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent], setting rash from false to true LOWERS acuity from emergent to self_care. This directly violates the monotonicity property "setting rash true never lowers acuity." The high-priority self_care override (r8) is the defect.
